sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Getafe has shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, while Levante UD has struggled with only 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses. Levante's recent heavy defeats indicate defensive vulnerabilities, whereas Getafe has been more resilient at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Levante UD won 3-2 in a high-scoring game, while the other match ended in a 0-0 draw. Matches between these teams often see goals and a high number of cards.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Getafe, positioned 7th with 10 points, has a balanced attack and defense, scoring 7 and conceding 8. Levante UD, in 17th with 4 points, has scored 10 but conceded 13, showing defensive frailties. Getafe's superior league standing and home advantage tilt the odds in their favor.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Trends
sections.text: Levante UD's games often feature over 2.5 goals (5/6 matches), while Getafe's matches frequently see over 4.5 cards (4/5). Both teams' head-to-head stats suggest high card counts and under 10.5 corners, influencing predictions for match events.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Levante UD leads Getafe 1-0 in a match that has seen contrasting fortunes for both sides at the Coliseum Alfonso Pérez. Levante took the lead through a well-taken goal by Iván Romero in the 26th minute, capitalizing on a clinical attacking move. Getafe, despite dominating possession, have struggled to create clear-cut chances and find a way past Levante's defense. The home side's frustration is evident with three yellow cards, indicating a lack of discipline under pressure. Levante, on the other hand, have been more effective in front of goal, creating several big chances, though they've missed a couple of notable opportunities to extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Getafe have controlled the ball for the majority of the first half but failed to convert their possession into meaningful opportunities. Their attacking play has lacked sharpness, with only a single shot on target despite numerous entries into the final third. Defensively, they've been solid but vulnerable to quick counter-attacks, as seen with Levante's goal. Levante, while having less of the ball, have been far more dangerous, creating multiple big chances and showing efficiency in their attacking transitions. Their defense has held firm, limiting Getafe's ability to penetrate the penalty area effectively, and they've also won possession in key areas to launch attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides, with both teams having periods of dominance in attack. Getafe started brightly, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes, but Levante quickly shifted the momentum with a series of threatening attacking spells around the early stages. The away side maintained pressure through consistent waves of attack, culminating in their goal. Getafe responded with renewed energy, mounting several attacking pushes around the midpoint of the half, but failed to sustain this as Levante regained control towards the end of the half with dangerous forays into Getafe's territory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Getafe, Borja Mayoral will be crucial in the second half if they are to turn the game around, as his ability to find space and create opportunities could unlock Levante's defense. Javier Muñoz also showed promise with a shot on target and could be a threat if given more freedom. On the Levante side, Iván Romero has been the standout performer with his goal and constant danger in attack, posing a significant threat with every forward move. Roger Brugué's creativity in setting up chances could also play a pivotal role in maintaining Levante's lead or extending it further.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Getafe will likely push for an equalizer early in the second half, focusing on utilizing their possession advantage to create more shots on goal. They may need to adjust their approach by taking more risks in attack, potentially leaving spaces at the back. Levante, with a lead to protect, might adopt a more cautious stance initially, looking to soak up pressure and hit on the counter. However, their ability to create big chances suggests they won't sit back entirely and will look to exploit any defensive lapses from Getafe.
